'''Kenneth C. Flint''' ([[June 23]], [[1940s#1947|1947]]—[[August 18]], [[2024]]) was an [[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] author. He contributed two short stories to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe: "[[Doctor Death: The Tale of Dr. Evazan and Ponda Baba]]" for ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]'' and "[[Old Friends: Ephant Mon's Tale]]" for ''[[Tales from Jabba's Palace]]''.

Flint was also the author of a novel in which [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] returned to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] in search of an artifact belonging to [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. The book, which [[Bantam Spectra]] titled ''[[Heart of the Jedi]]'', was eventually canceled.<ref name="Comics that Never Were">[[ComicScan|''ComicScan: Comics That Almost Were'']]</ref> Years later, a manuscript edited by him and [[Joseph Bongiorno|Joe Bongiorno]] was made available online,<ref name="EU Timeline">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarstimeline.net/The%20Heart%20of%20the%20Jedi.htm|text="Star Wars: The Heart of the Jedi"|work=[http://www.starwarstimeline.net The Star Wars Expanded Universe Timeline]|archivedate=20191230071348}}</ref> and a fan-made paperback version was briefly available via Amazon.com's self-publishing services, without the permission of [[Lucasfilm]].<ref name="Amazon">{{Amazon|url=Heart-Jedi-Kenneth-C-Flint/dp/B08Y55DZ23|text=The Heart of the Jedi|nolive=1}}</ref>
